narration: I have plotted a shortcut, past a dying star. When a sun like this collapses, it tears a hole in space itself. Most ships would be torn apart. But hidden in the data I recovered is a warning, spoken in a human voice. This is what silenced Earth. Hold on. We are going through.

shots.prompt: A swollen crimson supergiant star fills the sky, its surface boiling with vast plasma arcs and dark spots, pulsing as if breathing its last. The ship is a speck before it. Overwhelming scale.
The dying star collapses violently inward then erupts, a blinding shockwave of light and matter expanding outward across space. Dramatic slow-motion cosmic explosion.

A black hole forms from the collapse — a perfectly dark sphere ringed by a glowing orange-red accretion disk spiraling inward, starlight bending around it in a luminous ring. Terrifying and beautiful.
The starship threads dangerously close past the black hole, space and starlight visibly warping and stretching around the hull, gravitational lensing distorting the stars. White-knuckle tension.
